title: 50-feet-long fiber optic HDMI cable and Steam Controller 2 is enthusiasts' answer to the Steam Machine dismisses Valve's new console for a DIY Bazzite setup with a controller
summary: A gamer is creating a custom Steam Machine using a 50-feet-long fiber optic HDMI cable and a Steam Controller 2, opting for a DIY setup over Valve's official console.
insight: DIY approach offers customization and cost-effectiveness alternatives to official gaming consoles.

title: Sony crammed an entire PS1 into a DualShock controller that connects to your TV, but killed the project PlayStation Puga offered game studios a mere 10 cents per unit sold
summary: Sony developed a compact PS1 inside a controller, but cancelled due to low royalties for game studios.
insight: Low royalties threatened game studio profitability, derailed innovative product release.

title: Chinese YMTC SSDs make their way into retail Lenovo laptops media outlet slams YMTC PCIe 4.0 drive for 'below average for an SSD in an office laptop' in review
summary: Lenovo laptops now feature YMTC SSDs, despite US Entity List inclusion. The move marks a significant step for the Chinese storage chip company in gaining a presence in the US market. This development raises concerns about security and trade implications.
insight: Chinese tech companies are expanding US presence despite security concerns

title: Get a premium 27-inch 1440p 240 Hz OLED gaming monitor for only $349 OLED for the price of IPS
summary: Asus offers a 27-inch 1440p 240 Hz OLED gaming monitor at a budget-friendly price of $349. This premium display features an advanced proximity sensor that turns off the screen when not in use, mitigating burn-in concerns.
insight: Budget OLED gaming now accessible without compromising on performance

title: Meta data center water discharges suspended after contaminating the city's reclamation water supply with bacterium system offline for months for cleaning, closed-loop cooling system purge spread rare metal-resistant bacteria in Cheyenne s water system
summary: A meta data center's water discharge was suspended due to contamination of Cheyenne's reclamation water supply with a rare metal-resistant bacteria. The closed-loop cooling system's purge led to the issue, which required the system to be offline for months for cleaning. The incident involved a fill-and-flush commissioning step.
insight: Inadequate cooling system maintenance can contaminate entire city water systems.

title: AOC U27G4XM 27-inch 4K 160 Hz Dual-Refresh Gaming Monitor Review: Speed, Flexibility And Value
summary: The AOC U27G4XM offers a versatile 27-inch gaming monitor with 4K and FHD modes, 160 Hz refresh rate, and HDR capabilities.
insight: Exceptional speed and flexibility at an affordable price point offered

title: Memory price surge begins to cool as consumers hit affordability limit AI demand still keeps DRAM and NAND prices climbing through Q3 2026
summary: DRAM and NAND prices will continue to rise through Q3 2026, but at a slower pace due to consumer affordability limits. PC and smartphone makers are reaching their price ceilings, causing AI-driven demand growth to slow. This trend may lead to a stabilization in memory prices.
insight: Artificial intelligence demand slows as consumer affordability reaches limit.

title: Windows guru uses 19th-century Stirling Engine tech for auxiliary cooling on AMD Threadripper 3970X system waste heat energy spins the $40 engine's flywheel
summary: Windows development guru Dave Plummer demonstrates a Stirling Engine powered cooling solution for his AMD Threadripper system, utilizing waste heat energy.
insight: Repurposing waste heat energy for cooling in high-performance systems is efficient.

title: Enthusiast hides gaming PC inside living room fan using 3D-printed parts disassembled AtomMan G7 cooled by Dreo tower fan that shifts air at 28 feet per second
summary: A gaming PC enthusiast hid their mini-PC inside a living room fan using 3D-printed parts. The setup is cooled by a Dreo tower fan, which shifts air at 28 feet per second. This innovative solution conceals the PC in plain sight.
insight: Creative concealment of gaming hardware for a clutter-free living space.

title: Startup unveils 3D-printed nuclear reactor module to power AI data centers touted as the world s first subcritical, solid-state, factory-built thorium nuclear reactor
summary: Nuclear tech startup Ampera unveiled a 3D-printed small modular reactor, expected to be the world's first subcritical, solid-state, factory-built thorium nuclear reactor mass-produced for data centers and other applications.
insight: Game-changing nuclear energy innovation for sustainable AI data centers

title: Macintosh emulator works on ESP32-P4 display devkits from M5Stack and Waveshare
summary: Austin McChord ported BasiliskII to ESP32-P4 devkits, enabling classic Mac OS on portable devices with touchscreen, USB, and WiFi support. The emulator supports Motorola 68040 emulation with FPU and runs on M5Stack Tab5 and Waveshare 10.1-inch displays with scaled resolutions.
insight: Retro computing on portable, affordable ESP32-P4 embedded devices is now possible.

title: AI hallucinations in embedded are, yes, a thing
summary: Bob Martin, a Microchip Technology expert, shares his skepticism on vibe coding's challenges. He emphasizes the potential risks of AI hallucinations in embedded systems. This highlights the need for careful validation.
insight: AI hallucinations can lead to unreliable embedded system behavior.

title: Sensors are essential to robots, as Computex made clear
summary: At Computex, a robot barista demonstrated high-speed coffee production for attendees.
insight: Advanced sensors enable robots to perform complex and precise tasks.

title: The US now has three microreactors running and data centers are watching closely
summary: Three microreactors in the US have reached criticality, marking progress in nuclear energy.
insight: Accelerating advanced nuclear demonstrations for cleaner and more efficient energy.

title: SpaceX is reportedly showing investors a phone prototype, months after Musk said "we are not developing a phone"
summary: SpaceX is reportedly developing a phone despite earlier denials by Musk. The device is still in its early stages and design changes are possible. It's unclear if the product will reach the market.
insight: SpaceX's secrecy and changing plans raise investor trust concerns

title: Meta's Pocket app lets you vibe-code games with zero coding knowledge
summary: Meta's Pocket app allows users to create and share interactive mini-games without coding knowledge.
insight: Democratizing game development with user-friendly, no-code experiences.

title: Amazon's satellite internet service is finally ready for real users sort of
summary: Amazon's satellite internet service is nearing readiness after a beta phase, but it's still uneven. Early users experienced frequent interruptions and high sensitivity to obstructions. Speeds varied, but generally improved over time.
insight: Satellite internet is still developing, with room for significant improvement.
